headline_summary: U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran have escalated into a multi-day regional conflict with direct impacts on energy infrastructure and shipping.
Iran has declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and warned it will fire on any ship attempting to pass; multiple vessels have been hit.
Major producers and facilities have taken precautionary shutdowns (Qatar LNG, Saudi Ras Tanura refinery, Israeli gas fields, Iraqi Kurdistan output), tightening supply.
Container lines (Maersk, Hapag-Lloyd, CMA CGM, MSC) are rerouting/pausing Suez/Bab el-Mandeb transits and halting Hormuz crossings; war-risk surcharges and insurer cover cancellations add costs.
Oil, European gas and shipping rates have surged; broader global markets have moved risk-off amid inflation and growth concerns.
risk_drivers: Threatened/prospective closure of the Strait of Hormuz (critical chokepoint for oil/LNG)
Direct attacks on tankers and energy infrastructure across Gulf states
Precautionary shutdowns at major oil/gas facilities (Qatar LNG, Saudi refinery, Israeli gas fields, Iraqi production)
Spillover into Lebanon/Hezbollah front raising multi-theatre escalation risk
War-risk insurance withdrawal and extreme freight-rate repricing disrupting physical markets

Energy: Higher oil risk premium; potential for further upside if Hormuz closure persists or more Gulf infrastructure is hit.
European gas prices sensitive to LNG supply outages (Qatar) and shipping constraints; volatility elevated.
Refining/product spreads can widen if regional refineries/ports face disruptions (e.g., Ras Tanura, Fujairah).
FX: Risk-off flows can pressure EM FX, especially energy importers in Asia; safe-haven demand may support USD/JPY/CHF.
Gulf currency pegs may hold but local liquidity/credit conditions could tighten if disruption extends.
Rates: Higher energy prices raise near-term inflation risk, complicating DM central bank paths and lifting term premium.
Wider credit spreads possible on geopolitical shock + growth risk; funding costs rise for shipping/energy-linked credits.
Equities: Broad risk-off with underperformance in airlines/transport/chemicals; relative support for energy producers and some defence names.
Higher input and freight costs could hit margins across global industrial and consumer supply chains.

sources.title: US and Israel launch "pre-emptive" attack against Iran
Iran vows to attack any ship trying to pass through Strait of Hormuz
Qatar LNG, Saudi refinery, Israeli oil, gas fields down due to Mideast strikes
Shipping companies divert vessels around Cape of Good Hope after strikes on Iran
Global oil and gas shipping costs surge as Iran vows to close Strait of Hormuz
Iran war enters fourth day in 'smoke and blood' as markets slide
Oil rises as expanding US-Israeli conflict with Iran elevates supply risks
Exclusive: India reduces gas supply to industries after Qatar outage, sources say
Global energy costs soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, oil and gas production
Marine insurers cancel war risk cover, tanker costs to rise as Iran conflict intensifies

events.title: U.S. and Israel launch pre-emptive strikes on Iran
events.implication: Start of direct interstate escalation; increases probability of retaliatory strikes on Gulf energy assets and maritime chokepoints.

events.title: Iran vows to attack any ship attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Raises effective closure risk for a key oil/LNG chokepoint; likely to trigger rerouting, higher freight/insurance and supply delays.

events.title: Middle East oil and gas shipping costs surge to all-time high
events.implication: Freight spikes amplify delivered energy prices and tighten near-term supply by discouraging liftings and delaying cargoes.

events.title: Container lines pause/avoid Suez and Bab el-Mandeb; reroute via Cape of Good Hope
events.implication: Longer transit times and higher costs feed into global supply chains; adds a second chokepoint shock alongside Hormuz risk.

events.title: Saudi Aramco shuts Ras Tanura refinery after drone strike (source)
events.implication: Refining outage risks product shortages and wider crack spreads; highlights vulnerability of Gulf infrastructure.

events.title: Qatar LNG supply disrupted amid regional strikes
events.implication: LNG outage tightens Asian and European gas balances and raises spot LNG/TTF volatility.

events.title: Israeli oil and gas fields down due to regional strikes
events.implication: Local supply disruption can spill into Eastern Mediterranean gas flows and increases electricity/industrial risk.

events.title: Oil rises as expanding US-Israeli conflict with Iran elevates supply risks
events.implication: Market reprices higher risk premium; front-end supply fears can steepen backwardation and lift inflation hedges.

events.title: Global energy costs soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping and production
events.implication: Higher delivered costs act like a tax on growth and can drive risk-off positioning across assets.

events.title: Marine insurers cancel war-risk cover as Iran conflict intensifies
events.implication: Loss of cover forces voyage cancellations and raises effective freight costs; reinforces physical supply tightness.

events.title: OPEC+ agrees modest oil output boost even as conflict disrupts shipments
events.implication: Policy response could partially offset supply shock, but logistics constraints may limit near-term barrels reaching market.

events.title: ~10% of global container fleet caught in Hormuz backup (shipping CEO)
events.implication: Container congestion compounds rerouting effects, lifting spot rates and delaying intermediate goods.

events.title: Asian countries reliant on Middle East supply face oil disruption risks
events.implication: Elevates near-term demand for strategic stocks and alternative grades; pressures trade balances for importers.

events.title: India reduces gas supply to industries after Qatar outage (sources)
events.implication: Downstream rationing indicates real-economy spillover from LNG disruption; could hit industrial output.

events.title: Iran war enters fourth day as markets slide
events.implication: Protracted conflict increases tail risk of further infrastructure hits and broader regional involvement.

links.from: U.S. and Israel launch pre-emptive strikes on Iran
links.to: Iran vows to attack any ship attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz
links.why: Reuters reports the direct strikes preceded Iran's warnings against Hormuz transit as the conflict escalated.

links.from: Iran vows to attack any ship attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz
links.to: Middle East oil and gas shipping costs surge to all-time high
links.why: Threat/closure risk increases war-risk premia and reduces available tonnage, pushing freight rates higher.

links.from: Marine insurers cancel war-risk cover as Iran conflict intensifies
links.to: Middle East oil and gas shipping costs surge to all-time high
links.why: Loss of cover and higher premiums make voyages uneconomic for some owners, tightening supply of ships.

links.from: Iran vows to attack any ship attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz
links.why: Hormuz is a critical chokepoint for crude and LNG; credible closure risk lifts the oil risk premium.

links.from: Saudi Aramco shuts Ras Tanura refinery after drone strike (source)
links.why: Refinery outages and vulnerability of Gulf infrastructure add to supply/product disruption risk.
Higher oil prices worsen trade balances and inflation for net importers, often weakening EM FX.
Energy-driven inflation risk can lift yields/term premia and widen credit spreads during geopolitical shocks.

links.from: Global energy costs soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping and production
links.why: Rising energy and freight costs increase recession/inflation tradeoffs, prompting de-risking across assets.
